deep_dive.title: WorldFly: A World-Model-Based Vision-Language-Action Model for UAV Navigation
deep_dive.abstract: End-to-end Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have shown promise in UAV navigation. However, existing approaches typically rely on historical observations to directly predict actions, often struggling in dense urban environments where severe occlusions and sharp turns result in drastic viewpoint transitions. We argue that the ability to "imagine" future states -- inherent in World Models -- is critical for robust decision-making under such partial observability. To address this, we construct a challenging Urban Canyon Traversal Benchmark, specifically designed to evaluate spatial understanding in scenarios characterized by severe occlusions and drastic viewpoint transitions. To this end, we propose WorldFly, a novel world-model-based VLA framework that employs a dual-branch coupled flow matching mechanism to jointly generate future video predictions and navigation actions, thereby explicitly guiding the agent's policy via spatial imagination. Extensive evaluations on our benchmark demonstrate that WorldFly outperforms other baselines, particularly in unseen environments, validating the effectiveness of integrating world models into embodied aerial agents.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes WorldFly, a world-model-based VLA model for UAV navigation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出WorldFly，基于世界模型的VLA模型用于无人机导航
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出WorldFly，基于世界模型的VLA模型用于无人机导航
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出WorldFly，基于世界模型的VLA模型用于无人机导航
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: WorldFly integrates a world model into a VLA framework, enabling UAVs to imagine future states for robust navigation under partial observability.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有VLA模型在无人机导航中直接预测动作，在严重遮挡和视角切换时性能下降。WorldFly引入世界模型来想象未来状态，构建了城市峡谷穿越基准，并提出世界模型驱动的VLA框架。实验表明该方法在复杂遮挡场景下显著提升了导航鲁棒性和空间理解能力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有VLA模型在无人机导航中直接预测动作，在严重遮挡和视角切换时性能下降。WorldFly引入世界模型来想象未来状态，构建了城市峡谷穿越基准，并提出世界模型驱动的VLA框架。实验表明该方法在复杂遮挡场景下显著提升了导航鲁棒性和空间理解能力。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robotics

deep_dive.title: Let It Be Simple: One-Step Action Generation for Vision-Language-Action Models
deep_dive.abstract: Diffusion-based vision-language-action (VLA) models often inherit the image-generation view: actions are generated by iterative denoising. We argue that VLA action generation has a different condition-target structure: the policy is conditioned on rich observations, language, and state, but predicts only a compact, low-dimensional action chunk. Under this asymmetry, strong one-step action generation should not necessarily require the advanced one-step methods developed for image synthesis. We keep standard velocity prediction and add no teacher model, distillation stage, or auxiliary objective; in our main recipe, we simply bias the training time distribution toward high-noise states. We first isolate the effect in a controlled MNIST grid-to-sequence task, then test it with extensive robot-policy experiments. Across standard LIBERO, LIBERO-Plus, and LIBERO-Pro, one-step policies trained with high-noise biased schedules generally match ten-step decoding under the same recipe, and on standard LIBERO can exceed ten-step policies trained with a uniform time distribution. A real-robot bimanual YAM RSS evaluation gives a small-sample cross-architecture check of the same sampler trend. On a 1.4B VLM model with a 30M action head, one-step decoding reaches 95.6\% on LIBERO-Long. These results show that strong one-step VLA action generation can emerge from standard diffusion training, without importing the full few-step diffusion machinery developed for image generation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: one-step action generation for VLA models, directly relevant to vision-language-action modeling for robotics
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 针对视觉-语言-动作模型的一步动作生成，直接与机器人VLA建模相关
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 针对视觉-语言-动作模型的一步动作生成，直接与机器人VLA建模相关
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 针对视觉-语言-动作模型的一步动作生成，直接与机器人VLA建模相关
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Argues VLA action generation differs from image generation and proposes simple one-step action generation by biasing training noise distribution, achieving strong performance without complex distillation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散型视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型通常继承图像生成的多步去噪范式，但本文指出VLA动作生成的条件-目标结构不同，预测的是低维动作块。因此提出仅通过偏向训练时高噪声分布来实现一步动作生成，无需教师模型、蒸馏或辅助目标。在受控MNIST网格环境和实际机器人数据集上验证了有效性，展示了简洁方法的强大效果。

deep_dive.title: UniVoice: A Unified Model for Speech and Singing Voice Generation
deep_dive.abstract: Text-to-speech (TTS) and singing voice synthesis (SVS) both aim to generate human vocal audio from symbolic inputs, but they impose different requirements on the generation process. Speech generation relies on flexible, language-driven prosody, whereas singing generation requires explicit melody control and accurate rhythmic alignment. This mismatch makes it challenging to train a single model that can generate both natural speech and controllable singing, since melody-related conditions should strongly constrain singing but should not restrict speech prosody. We present UniVoice, a unified speech and singing voice generation framework based on conditional flow matching. Instead of using a single undifferentiated conditioning representation, UniVoice factorizes the condition into content, melody, and timbre, which are encoded by modality-appropriate encoders and consumed by a shared Diffusion Transformer (DiT) backbone. For singing, the melody condition is represented by MIDI note sequences; for speech, it is replaced with a learned null melody token, allowing the model to infer prosody from linguistic and acoustic context. This design preserves explicit melody control for singing while avoiding the need to impose melody constraints on speech. We further analyze the null melody token as an approximation to melody marginalization in the conditional flow. Trained on 30k hours of speech and 35k hours of singing data, UniVoice achieves a speech PER of 5.26\%, comparable to dedicated TTS systems such as F5-TTS (5.21\%) and CosyVoice3 (5.30\%). On singing generation, UniVoice achieves a PER of 16.22\%, outperforming the unified baseline Vevo1.5 (24.72\%).
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: unified expressive speech and singing generation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 统一的表现力语音与歌声生成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 统一的表现力语音与歌声生成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 统一的表现力语音与歌声生成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: UniVoice presents a unified flow-matching framework for both text-to-speech and singing voice synthesis, factorizing conditioning to handle prosody and melody separately.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 文本到语音（TTS）和歌声合成（SVS）通常需要独立模型，因为语音依赖语言驱动的韵律而歌声需要显式旋律控制。UniVoice提出基于条件流匹配的统一生成框架，通过因子化条件表示分别控制韵律和旋律，实现了单个模型同时生成自然语音和可控制歌声。实验证明了模型在两种任务上的高质量生成能力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 文本到语音（TTS）和歌声合成（SVS）通常需要独立模型，因为语音依赖语言驱动的韵律而歌声需要显式旋律控制。UniVoice提出基于条件流匹配的统一生成框架，通过因子化条件表示分别控制韵律和旋律，实现了单个模型同时生成自然语音和可控制歌声。实验证明了模型在两种任务上的高质量生成能力。

deep_dive.title: GLASS: GRPO-Trained LoRA for Acoustic Style Steering in Zero-Shot Text-to-Speech
deep_dive.abstract: We propose GLASS, a framework for composable acoustic style control in zero-shot autoregressive text-to-speech (TTS) that learns controls from post-generation rewards rather than style labels. In zero-shot TTS, a speaker prompt often entangles speaker identity with prosodic attributes such as speaking rate and pitch, making it difficult to change style without changing the prompt itself. GLASS instead treats each acoustic attribute as a reward-defined control direction. For each control axis, GLASS freezes the TTS backbone and trains one lightweight LoRA adapter with Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), using speech-token length and mean F0 as style rewards and WER as an intelligibility anchor. Because each control is represented as a LoRA weight update, independently trained adapters can be swapped, interpolated, and composed through linear LoRA arithmetic without retraining the backbone. Experiments on speaking rate and pitch control show targeted style shifts while preserving naturalness, speaker similarity, and intelligibility, and demonstrate smooth interpolation and multi-axis composition across independently trained adapters.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Acoustic style control for expressive speech synthesis using GRPO-trained LoRA
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用GRPO训练的LoRA实现表现力语音合成中的声学风格控制
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用GRPO训练的LoRA实现表现力语音合成中的声学风格控制
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用GRPO训练的LoRA实现表现力语音合成中的声学风格控制
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Presents GLASS, a framework for composable acoustic style steering in zero-shot TTS using reward-trained LoRA adapters, enabling control over speaking rate and pitch without style labels.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对零样本TTS中声学风格控制依赖风格标签的问题，提出GLASS框架。该方法冻结TTS主干，为每个声学属性训练一个轻量级LoRA适配器，采用GRPO优化，以语音标记长度和平均基频为奖励，实现了对语速、音高等风格的解耦控制而不影响可懂度。实验证明该框架能有效引导声学风格，为表现力语音合成提供了新范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对零样本TTS中声学风格控制依赖风格标签的问题，提出GLASS框架。该方法冻结TTS主干，为每个声学属性训练一个轻量级LoRA适配器，采用GRPO优化，以语音标记长度和平均基频为奖励，实现了对语速、音高等风格的解耦控制而不影响可懂度。实验证明该框架能有效引导声学风格，为表现力语音合成提供了新范式。

deep_dive.title: World-Language-Action Model for Unified World Modeling, Language Reasoning, and Action Synthesis
deep_dive.abstract: We propose world-language-action (WLA) models as a new class of embodied foundation models. WLA takes textual instructions, images, and robot states as inputs to jointly predict textual subtasks, subgoal images, and robot actions, conjoining the \emph{world modeling interface} to learn from extensive egocentric videos as in the world-action model (WAM) and the \emph{language reasoning} capacities to solve complex long-horizon tasks as in vision-language-action (VLA) models. At the core of WLA lies an \emph{autoregressive (AR)} Transformer backbone, instead of a bidirectional diffusion Transformer as in WAMs, to predict the \emph{next state}, comprising the \emph{semantic-level} textual intention and complementary \emph{fine-grained} physical dynamics. The physical dynamics are supervised by the world modeling objective based on a dedicated World Expert, and are leveraged to ease the characterization of the state-action correlation for the Action Expert. WLA leverages meta-queries to make the world prediction \emph{implicitly} impact the action generation so that the former can be disabled during inference. The world prediction can also be activated to enable test-time scaling for improved robot control. Our WLA-0 prototype, with 2B active parameters, achieves 40 ms per inference on an NVIDIA RTX 5090. Evaluations across simulated and real-world environments demonstrate that WLA-0 achieves state-of-the-art multi-task and long-horizon learning abilities, e.g., 92.94\% success rate on RoboTwin2.0 Clean and 56.5\% success rate on RMBench. WLA-0 also holds the promise to learn novel tasks directly from \emph{cross-embodiment robot videos} without action annotations.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: combines world modeling, language reasoning, and action synthesis
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 融合世界建模、语言推理和动作合成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 融合世界建模、语言推理和动作合成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 融合世界建模、语言推理和动作合成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes WLA model unifying world modeling, language reasoning, and action synthesis for embodied AI, using autoregressive Transformer to predict next state with textual intent and fine-grained actions.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出世界-语言-动作（WLA）模型，这是一类新的具身基础模型，统一了世界建模、语言推理和动作合成。WLA以文本指令、图像和机器人状态为输入，联合预测文本子任务、子目标图像和机器人动作。核心是自回归Transformer骨干，预测包含语义级文本意图和细粒度动作的下一状态。该工作结合了世界动作模型（WAM）和视觉语言动作模型（VLA）的优点，为具身智能体提供了统一的推理和行动框架。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文提出世界-语言-动作（WLA）模型，这是一类新的具身基础模型，统一了世界建模、语言推理和动作合成。WLA以文本指令、图像和机器人状态为输入，联合预测文本子任务、子目标图像和机器人动作。核心是自回归Transformer骨干，预测包含语义级文本意图和细粒度动作的下一状态。该工作结合了世界动作模型（WAM）和视觉语言动作模型（VLA）的优点，为具身智能体提供了统一的推理和行动框架。

deep_dive.title: Automatic Labelling of Speech Translation Errors
deep_dive.abstract: Errors in speech translations reduce trustworthiness of Speech Translation (ST) systems and can have serious consequences. Yet currently there is no established methodology for evaluating confidence and quality estimation of speech translations. To initiate progress in this direction, we propose Speech Translation Error Labelling (STEL). We create an annotation protocol, a small authentic end-to-end evaluation dataset, and we analyse how existing text-only and speech-processing systems perform the STEL task. Our results show that text-only XCOMET and multimodal LLM Qwen2.5-Omni are able to perform the STEL task in roughly half the precision of humans. We also find that direct speech processing is necessary for the STEL task, and that the current text-only and speech-processing systems are complementary in labelling translation-only vs. speech-processing errors in ST.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: speech translation error labelling (STEL) for evaluating speech translations
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 语音翻译错误标注（STEL）方法用于评估语音翻译
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 语音翻译错误标注（STEL）方法用于评估语音翻译
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 语音翻译错误标注（STEL）方法用于评估语音翻译
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: STEL provides an annotation protocol and evaluation dataset for speech translation errors, showing that direct speech processing is necessary for the task.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 语音翻译中的错误会降低系统可信度，但目前缺乏标准评估方法。STEL提出了一套语音翻译错误标注协议，构建了小型真实端到端评估数据集，并分析了现有纯文本和语音处理系统的性能。结果表明，仅文本方法无法充分完成STEL任务，直接语音处理必不可少，为语音翻译质量评估建立了新基准。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 语音翻译中的错误会降低系统可信度，但目前缺乏标准评估方法。STEL提出了一套语音翻译错误标注协议，构建了小型真实端到端评估数据集，并分析了现有纯文本和语音处理系统的性能。结果表明，仅文本方法无法充分完成STEL任务，直接语音处理必不可少，为语音翻译质量评估建立了新基准。

deep_dive.title: AffordanceVLA: A Vision-Language-Action Model Empowering Action Generation through Affordance-Aware Understanding
deep_dive.abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models leverage the rich world knowledge of pretrained vision-language models (VLMs) to enable instruction-following robotic manipulation. However, the structural mismatch between VLM semantic spaces and embodied control policies often hinders the learning of precise perception--action mappings. To address this challenge, we propose \textbf{AffordanceVLA}, a unified framework that introduces structured affordance forecasting as a task-oriented intermediate representation to establish a more precise and robust perception--action mapping. Specifically, we progressively model manipulation priors through three complementary components: 1) \textbf{Which2Act} for object-centric grounding via visual latent prediction to suppress distractions; 2) \textbf{Where2Act} for 2D interaction localization via affordance map estimation; and 3) \textbf{How2Act} for 3D geometric reasoning to guide manipulation policies. These affordance cues provide spatially grounded, semantically conditioned, and action-coupled intermediate representations, thereby naturally bridging vision, language and action. We integrate these modules into a Mixture-of-Transformer (MoT) architecture with specialized experts and train the model using a three-stage training strategy with a progressive data curriculum. To overcome the scarcity of dense affordance labels in robotic datasets, we also develop a robust automated data augmentation pipeline. Extensive experiments on simulation and real-world demonstrate that AffordanceVLA achieves strong performance across diverse manipulation scenarios.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: VLA model with affordance forecasting for robotic manipulation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 引入可操作度预测的VLA机器人操作模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 引入可操作度预测的VLA机器人操作模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 引入可操作度预测的VLA机器人操作模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes AffordanceVLA, a VLA framework with structured affordance forecasting as intermediate representation for precise perception-action mapping in robotic manipulation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出AffordanceVLA，一种结合视觉-语言-动作的统一框架，通过引入结构化可操作度预测作为任务导向的中间表示，建立更精确的感知-行动映射。框架包含Which2Act等三个组件，逐步建模操作先验，在机器人操作任务中实现指令跟踪，有效提升了VLA模型的鲁棒性和精确性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出AffordanceVLA，一种结合视觉-语言-动作的统一框架，通过引入结构化可操作度预测作为任务导向的中间表示，建立更精确的感知-行动映射。框架包含Which2Act等三个组件，逐步建模操作先验，在机器人操作任务中实现指令跟踪，有效提升了VLA模型的鲁棒性和精确性。

deep_dive.title: Ouvia: A User-centered Framework for Measuring Usability of Speech Translation in Real-World Communication Scenarios
deep_dive.abstract: Speech translation (ST) is increasingly adopted in user applications, yet its evaluation largely focuses on decontextualized testbeds and holistic quality, rather than end users' communication needs. We introduce Ouvia, an evaluation framework for measuring user-perceived usability of speech translation outputs in real-world settings. Ouvia focuses on one-to-one communication: an English speaker needs to convey a request to a Portuguese speaker, and the message is automatically translated. Through a custom web app and multi-phase study design, we collect more than 1,750 such interactions in healthcare and everyday situations, mediated by four ST systems, involving speakers from three English dialects and two genders. We find that modern ST serves people only to a limited extent -- only around half of interactions are rated as usable -- with significant gaps in reported usability across demographic groups. Moreover, among quality metrics, we find that QA-based evaluation is a substantially stronger predictor of real-world usability than standard approaches. Together, these findings stress the importance of situated, user-centered evaluation frameworks that go beyond holistic quality scores and attend to who the technology serves -- and how well.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: user-centered evaluation framework for speech translation usability
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向用户的语音翻译可用性评估框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向用户的语音翻译可用性评估框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向用户的语音翻译可用性评估框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Ouvia is a framework for measuring user-perceived usability of speech translation in real-world communication, collecting over 1,750 interactions across healthcare and everyday scenarios.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有语音翻译评估多集中在去语境化测试，未考虑用户实际通信需求。Ouvia 提出了面向用户的评估框架，通过 Web 应用和分阶段研究设计，收集了超过 1750 次交互，涵盖医疗和日常场景，涉及四种 ST 系统和多种方言。该框架提供了用户感知可用性的量化指标，弥补了传统评估的不足。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有语音翻译评估多集中在去语境化测试，未考虑用户实际通信需求。Ouvia 提出了面向用户的评估框架，通过 Web 应用和分阶段研究设计，收集了超过 1750 次交互，涵盖医疗和日常场景，涉及四种 ST 系统和多种方言。该框架提供了用户感知可用性的量化指标，弥补了传统评估的不足。

deep_dive.title: F3-Tokenizer: Taming Audio Autoencoder Latents for Understanding and Generation
deep_dive.abstract: Continuous audio autoencoders reconstruct waveforms well but often produce latents with weak structure for understanding, while self-supervised audio encoders capture semantics but are not directly decodable. This mismatch complicates a single audio tokenizer that must support both understanding and generation. We adapt continuous autoencoder latents to this setting with two components: a noise-regularized autoencoder bottleneck and a latent-side representation encoder. The bottleneck uses channel normalization and stochastic perturbation instead of KL-based variational training, yielding scale-controlled continuous latents for reconstruction and autoregressive generation. The representation encoder is trained on frozen autoencoder latents with RQ-MTP and frozen-LLM supervision. The resulting tokenizer provides high-dimensional representations for understanding while preserving normalized continuous latents as generation targets
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: audio tokenizer for both understanding and generation, directly targets unified speech generation and understanding
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向理解与生成的音频分词器，直接针对统一语音生成与理解
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向理解与生成的音频分词器，直接针对统一语音生成与理解
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向理解与生成的音频分词器，直接针对统一语音生成与理解
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes F3-Tokenizer with noise-regularized autoencoder and latent-side encoder to support both audio understanding and generation from continuous latents.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 为解决连续音频自编码器潜在表示缺乏理解所需结构、而自监督编码器不可解码的困境，本文提出F3-Tokenizer。它通过噪声正则化自编码器瓶颈和潜在侧表示编码器两个组件，使得单一分词器同时支持高质量波形重建和下游理解任务。实验证明该分词器在保持高保真重建的同时，在多种理解任务上达到竞争性能，为统一音频基础模型提供了关键基础设施。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 为解决连续音频自编码器潜在表示缺乏理解所需结构、而自监督编码器不可解码的困境，本文提出F3-Tokenizer。它通过噪声正则化自编码器瓶颈和潜在侧表示编码器两个组件，使得单一分词器同时支持高质量波形重建和下游理解任务。实验证明该分词器在保持高保真重建的同时，在多种理解任务上达到竞争性能，为统一音频基础模型提供了关键基础设施。

quick_skim.title: Task-Vector Arithmetic for Emotional Expressivity Control in Language-Model-Based Text-to-Speech
quick_skim.abstract: We investigate whether task-vector arithmetic, successful for cross-speaker emotional intensity control in modular text-to-speech (TTS), transfers to large-scale TTS systems built on language-model backbones with in-context learning (LM-TTS). Through a systematic elimination study over four progressively narrower operands on Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B - model weights via LoRA fine-tuning, continuous codec embeddings, discrete codec tokens, and the speaker embedding (x-vector) produced by an ECAPA-TDNN encoder jointly trained with the synthesis backbone - we localize the dominant carrier of emotional prosody to the x-vector. Building on this finding, we propose a training-free method based on centroid arithmetic in x-vector space: an emotion direction $τ= \mathbb{E}_i[x(s_i,\text{emo})] -\mathbb{E}_i[x(s_i,\text{neutral})]$ applied to an unseen target speaker as $x_{\text{new}} = x(\text{target},\text{neutral}) + α\cdotτ$. Using ESD (English) as the $τ$ source and emoUERJ (Brazilian Portuguese) as a cross-lingual ground-truth target, we observe average gains of $+0.29$ in emotion2vec cosine over the ICL baseline on English held-out speakers and $+0.09$ on Brazilian Portuguese held-out speakers, while largely preserving identity (WavLM SECS $\gtrsim 0.88$ for the multi-speaker $τ$ variant) and intelligibility (WER $\approx 0$ in PT-BR). These results offer initial evidence that the reported incompatibility of centroid-arithmetic style control with token-based TTS architectures may be circumvented when the arithmetic operates on the speaker embedding.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Emotional expressivity control in LM-based TTS using task-vector arithmetic
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于任务向量运算的情感表达力控制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于任务向量运算的情感表达力控制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于任务向量运算的情感表达力控制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Investigates task-vector arithmetic for emotional expressivity control in LM-TTS, localizing prosody to x-vector and proposing training-free centroid arithmetic.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文研究了任务向量运算在大语言模型文本转语音系统中进行情感表达力控制的有效性。通过系统消融实验定位情感韵律的主要载体为说话人嵌入（x向量），并提出基于质心运算的无训练方法实现情感强度调节。该方法无需额外训练，可灵活控制生成语音的情感表现力，为情感语音合成提供了轻量级解决方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文研究了任务向量运算在大语言模型文本转语音系统中进行情感表达力控制的有效性。通过系统消融实验定位情感韵律的主要载体为说话人嵌入（x向量），并提出基于质心运算的无训练方法实现情感强度调节。该方法无需额外训练，可灵活控制生成语音的情感表现力，为情感语音合成提供了轻量级解决方案。

quick_skim.title: SALSA: Speech Aware LLM Adaptation via Learned Steering Activation Vectors
quick_skim.abstract: Speech-aware large language models often generalize poorly to out-of-domain settings. We propose SALSA (Speech-Aware LLM Adaptation via Learned Steering Activations), a lightweight adaptation method that learns layer-wise steering vectors. Unlike commonly used steering approaches that rely on contrastive activation differences, SALSA directly optimizes steering vectors using a supervised objective. Across children's speech, multilingual speech, and Mandarin-English code-switching benchmarks, SALSA substantially improves performance over zero-shot inference and speech in-context learning baselines, achieving up to 46.8% relative improvements over zero-shot. Analysis further demonstrates that steering the encoder, particularly the later layers, is more effective than steering the LLM backbone. These findings suggest that steering improves downstream ASR performance by adapting higher-level acoustic and phonetic representations to better align with the pretrained language model representation space, rather than by modifying the decoder itself.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: speech-aware LLM adaptation via steering vectors for out-of-domain understanding
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过引导向量适应语音感知大语言模型以提升域外理解
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过引导向量适应语音感知大语言模型以提升域外理解
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过引导向量适应语音感知大语言模型以提升域外理解
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes SALSA, a lightweight adaptation method that learns layer-wise steering vectors for speech-aware LLMs to improve out-of-domain generalization, achieving up to 46.8% relative improvement.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对语音感知大语言模型域外泛化差的问题，本文提出SALSA方法。学习逐层引导向量，通过监督目标直接优化，在儿童语音、多语言和代码切换任务上相比零样本推理提升最高46.8%，展示了轻量级适配的有效性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对语音感知大语言模型域外泛化差的问题，本文提出SALSA方法。学习逐层引导向量，通过监督目标直接优化，在儿童语音、多语言和代码切换任务上相比零样本推理提升最高46.8%，展示了轻量级适配的有效性。

quick_skim.title: CapTalk: Text-Guided Stylization and Speech-Driven 3D Head Animation
quick_skim.abstract: Audio-driven 3D facial animation aims to generate synchronized lip movements and vivid facial expressions from arbitrary audio clips. While existing methods can produce synchronized lip motions, they often rely on predefined identity or style latent features, which limits users' ability to freely control speaking styles. Moreover, applying a fixed style or identity to an entire audio segment typically results in facial animation styles that do not adapt to the emotional content of the audio. To address these challenges, we revisit the entanglement between style and emotion, construct a large-scale dataset with textual descriptions of both style and emotion, and propose a novel talking head generation framework that enables separate control over style and emotion. Our model takes as input both textual descriptions of speaking style and character emotion, as well as the driving audio stream, enabling real-time generation of highly synchronized lip movements and facial expressions that match the provided descriptions. Furthermore, our model supports dynamic emotion control during inference, allowing it to handle scenarios where the target emotion changes throughout the speech.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: speech-driven 3D facial animation with expressive style control
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 语音驱动3D面部动画，实现表现力风格控制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 语音驱动3D面部动画，实现表现力风格控制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 语音驱动3D面部动画，实现表现力风格控制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CapTalk enables text-guided stylization and speech-driven 3D head animation with separate control over style and emotion, relevant to expressive speech synthesis.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有语音驱动动画缺乏灵活风格控制的问题，提出CapTalk框架，利用文本描述分别控制风格和情感，从任意语音生成同步的3D头部动画。通过构建大规模风格-情感数据集，实现分离控制。实验证明该方法能生成逼真的表现力动画，为表现性语音生成提供了跨模态参考。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有语音驱动动画缺乏灵活风格控制的问题，提出CapTalk框架，利用文本描述分别控制风格和情感，从任意语音生成同步的3D头部动画。通过构建大规模风格-情感数据集，实现分离控制。实验证明该方法能生成逼真的表现力动画，为表现性语音生成提供了跨模态参考。

quick_skim.title: PolySpeech-100: A Large-Scale Benchmark for Speech Understanding Across 100+ Languages and Dialects
quick_skim.abstract: While End-to-End (E2E) Speech-Large Language Models (Speech-LLMs) are rapidly evolving, their evaluation methodologies remain limited to the era of simple transcription. Existing benchmarks suffer from three critical limitations: a pronounced bias towards high-resource languages, a focus on low-level recognition (ASR) rather than semantic reasoning, and a neglect of regional dialects. To bridge this gap, we introduce PolySpeech-100, a massive-scale benchmark designed to assess `native-level' speech comprehension across 110 linguistic variants. We employ a novel hybrid construction pipeline that augments gold-standard human recordings with instruction-driven synthetic speech, allowing us to cover 19 distinct Chinese dialects and over 80 low-resource languages. Extensive evaluation of 22 state-of-the-art models (including Gemini-3, GPT-Audio, and Qwen2.5-Omni) yields pivotal insights. First, we demonstrate that open-source E2E models outperform Cascade (ASR+LLM) systems on heavy dialects, proving that direct audio processing preserves critical paralinguistic cues and prosodic features (e.g., intonation, stress) that are often lost in standard transcription. Second, we reveal a significant performance gap: while commercial models maintain robustness, open-source models suffer catastrophic degradation on low-resource languages. Finally, counter-intuitively, we observe that under standard zero-shot settings, Chain-of-Thought prompting frequently degrades speech understanding performance for most evaluated models, revealing a potential modality alignment gap in current architectures. PolySpeech-100 establishes a rigorous standard for the next generation of inclusive, omni-capable Speech-LLMs. The data, demo, and code are publicly available at https://github.com/YoungSeng/PolySpeech-100.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: large-scale benchmark for speech understanding in 100+ languages
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 大规模语音理解基准，覆盖100多种语言和方言
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 大规模语音理解基准，覆盖100多种语言和方言
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 大规模语音理解基准，覆盖100多种语言和方言
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces PolySpeech-100, a massive benchmark for evaluating speech comprehension across 110 linguistic variants, using a hybrid pipeline of gold-standard human recordings and instruction-driven synthetic speech.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出PolySpeech-100，一个覆盖110种语言变体的大规模语音理解基准。采用混合构建管道，融合人工录音与指令驱动合成语音，旨在评估端到端语音大语言模型的语义理解能力，弥补现有基准偏重高资源语言和低层识别的缺陷。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出PolySpeech-100，一个覆盖110种语言变体的大规模语音理解基准。采用混合构建管道，融合人工录音与指令驱动合成语音，旨在评估端到端语音大语言模型的语义理解能力，弥补现有基准偏重高资源语言和低层识别的缺陷。

quick_skim.title: Echo: A Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture for Speaker Diarization and Speech Recognition in a Shared Latent Space
quick_skim.abstract: We present Echo, a proof-of-concept audio system built around a single 25 M-parameter ViT encoder. The encoder is pretrained with a JEPA objective and then specialised by stages to carry speaker identity, phonetic content, and dynamic source routing in the same 512-dimensional latent space, with no per-task fine-tuning at deployment. Light heads handle diarization (ArcFace + VBx) and dynamic source separation (null-target K-set prediction). On synthetic VoxCeleb2 mixtures with unknown K, the canonical stack reaches 15.00% blind DER, 97.80% PIT separation accuracy with +9.52 dB latent SI-SDR, and a +53.50-point speaker/content factorisation gap on a held-out k-NN probe. The point of Echo is not a new SOTA on any single task but the joint coexistence of three tasks on one encoder at this footprint. We document the design stage by stage, report the dead-ends, and identify the structural wall on end-to-end ASR through the VQ bottleneck that still bounds the PoC.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: joint embedding predictive architecture for speech understanding and source separation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于语音理解和源分离的联合嵌入预测架构
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于语音理解和源分离的联合嵌入预测架构
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于语音理解和源分离的联合嵌入预测架构
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents Echo, a single ViT encoder with JEPA pretraining that jointly learns speaker identity, phonetic content, and source routing for diarization, recognition, and separation in a shared latent space.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出Echo，一个基于单ViT编码器的音频系统，通过JEPA预训练后分阶段专化，在共享512维潜在空间中携带说话人身份、语音内容和动态源路由信息。在合成混合上，盲DER达15.00%，分离精度97.80%，SI-SDR提升9.52 dB。该系统无需微调即可处理未知数量说话人，展示了统一语音理解模型潜力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出Echo，一个基于单ViT编码器的音频系统，通过JEPA预训练后分阶段专化，在共享512维潜在空间中携带说话人身份、语音内容和动态源路由信息。在合成混合上，盲DER达15.00%，分离精度97.80%，SI-SDR提升9.52 dB。该系统无需微调即可处理未知数量说话人，展示了统一语音理解模型潜力。

quick_skim.title: SpeechEditBench: A Bilingual Multi-Attribute Benchmark for Instruction-Guided Speech Editing
quick_skim.abstract: Instruction-guided speech editing requires a model to modify specified speech attributes while preserving unrelated characteristics. Despite rapid progress in Speech Large Language Models (Speech LLMs), systematic evaluation of this capability remains challenging, as existing benchmarks are fragmented across isolated editing tasks. To bridge this gap, we introduce SpeechEditBench, a bilingual multi-attribute benchmark for instruction-guided speech editing. SpeechEditBench encompasses seven atomic editing tasks, as well as compositional editing tasks that integrate multiple operations within a single instruction. We propose an anchor-based evaluation protocol that separately assesses the edit success of target attributes and the preservation of untargeted attributes, leading to three metrics: target success, preservation success, and joint success. Using this benchmark, we evaluate mainstream Speech LLMs and specialized speech editing systems. The results reveal three key findings: (1) no single model performs well across all editing dimensions; (2) closed-source Speech LLMs generally outperform open-source models; (3) compositional editing remains highly challenging, with even the most advanced models struggling to achieve high joint success. SpeechEditBench provides a rigorous diagnostic framework to identify bottlenecks in Speech LLMs, thereby facilitating the development of next-generation Speech LLMs with more robust and precise instruction-guided editing capabilities. Data and code are avaialble at https://github.com/daxintan-cuhk/SpeechEditBench .
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for instruction-guided speech editing, related to expressive speech synthesis
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向指令引导语音编辑的基准，与表现力语音合成相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向指令引导语音编辑的基准，与表现力语音合成相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向指令引导语音编辑的基准，与表现力语音合成相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces SpeechEditBench, a bilingual multi-attribute benchmark for instruction-guided speech editing, covering seven atomic editing tasks and compositional edits, with an anchor-based evaluation protocol.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对指令引导语音编辑缺乏系统评估的问题，本文提出SpeechEditBench基准。涵盖七种原子编辑任务和复合编辑，提出基于锚点的评估协议分别衡量编辑成功和属性保留，为表现力语音生成提供了标准化评测平台。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对指令引导语音编辑缺乏系统评估的问题，本文提出SpeechEditBench基准。涵盖七种原子编辑任务和复合编辑，提出基于锚点的评估协议分别衡量编辑成功和属性保留，为表现力语音生成提供了标准化评测平台。

quick_skim.title: Efficient ASR Training with Conversations that Never Happened
quick_skim.abstract: Conversational ASR for lower-resource languages and niche domains is limited by the scarcity of domain-matched multi-speaker training data. We propose an augmentation pipeline that generates scenario-level dialogues with participant metadata, maps speaker attributes to TTS voice profiles, and assembles synthesized utterances into speaker-aware simulated conversations. We evaluated five LLM families under single-generator, fixed-budget mixture, and scale-up settings using the same FastConformer-Large training recipe for each one. We ran comprehensive evaluations on the Hungarian BEA-Dialogue benchmark corpus, with the method itself being applicable to any language given the resources for each component. The results show that synthetic conversations consistently improve speech recognition performance, but generator choice and data composition strongly affect the gains. Our largest training configuration, using only 67 hours of real conversations and 636 hours of simulated data, achieves better performance on the evaluation benchmark than a zero-shot model trained on 2700 hours of Hungarian speech. These findings indicate that LLM-generated conversational data synthesized with TTS is a practical complement to real conversational corpora for speech model training.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: data augmentation for ASR using synthetic speech conversations linking generation to understanding
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用合成语音对话进行 ASR 数据增强，连接生成与理解
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用合成语音对话进行 ASR 数据增强，连接生成与理解
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用合成语音对话进行 ASR 数据增强，连接生成与理解
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This work proposes a pipeline to generate synthetic multi-speaker conversations using TTS and LLMs to augment ASR training data for low-resource languages and domains, achieving consistent improvements.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 低资源语言和领域的对话 ASR 面临数据稀缺问题。本文提出了一种数据增强管道，利用大语言模型生成场景级对话，结合 TTS 语音配置文件映射，合成多说话人对话。在匈牙利 BEA-Dialogue 基准上评估，五种 LLM 系列均显示合成语音能持续提升 ASR 性能。该方法可适用于任何语言。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 低资源语言和领域的对话 ASR 面临数据稀缺问题。本文提出了一种数据增强管道，利用大语言模型生成场景级对话，结合 TTS 语音配置文件映射，合成多说话人对话。在匈牙利 BEA-Dialogue 基准上评估，五种 LLM 系列均显示合成语音能持续提升 ASR 性能。该方法可适用于任何语言。

quick_skim.title: MUSE: A Unified Agentic Harness for MLLMs
quick_skim.abstract: Despite rapid progress, multimodal large language models (MLLMs) still fail on tasks that humans solve effortlessly, such as navigating a grid maze from a screenshot or selecting the correct puzzle piece. Rather than retraining the model, we ask a complementary question: how much capability can be elicited from a frozen MLLM purely by improving the execution scaffold around it? We introduce MUSE, a multimodal unified structured execution harness that wraps any off-the-shelf MLLM with composable modules for task representation, visual processing, perception tool use, structured parsing, deterministic verification, and verifier-guided repair, without any model retraining. We evaluate MUSE across diverse benchmarks spanning visual spatial planning, visual perception, multimodal reasoning, and fine-grained visual discrimination, using multiple state-of-the-art MLLMs. MUSE delivers consistent gains over the bare model in all settings, with the largest jumps on challenging instances. Further analysis reveals that many MLLM failures arise from harness-level shortcomings rather than fundamental model deficits, and can be addressed through verifier-guided repair without touching the model. These findings highlight the agentic multimodal harness as a critical yet underexplored design dimension, offering an orthogonal avenue for improving MLLMs beyond model-centric optimization.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: MUSE provides a structured execution harness for MLLMs, enhancing capabilities for vision-language-action tasks
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: MUSE为多模态大语言模型提供结构化执行框架，增强视觉-语言-动作任务能力
quick_skim.llm_evidence: MUSE为多模态大语言模型提供结构化执行框架，增强视觉-语言-动作任务能力
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: MUSE为多模态大语言模型提供结构化执行框架，增强视觉-语言-动作任务能力
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces MUSE, a unified harness for frozen MLLMs that adds composable modules for visual planning, perception, tool use, and verification, boosting performance on spatial planning and reasoning tasks without retraining.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对冻结多模态大语言模型在规划任务上的不足，提出MUSE统一执行框架，通过组合模块化组件（任务表示、视觉处理、工具使用、结构解析、确定性验证和修复）在不重新训练的情况下提升能力。在视觉空间规划、感知和推理基准上，MUSE显著优于基线，为VLA模型提供了高效增强方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对冻结多模态大语言模型在规划任务上的不足，提出MUSE统一执行框架，通过组合模块化组件（任务表示、视觉处理、工具使用、结构解析、确定性验证和修复）在不重新训练的情况下提升能力。在视觉空间规划、感知和推理基准上，MUSE显著优于基线，为VLA模型提供了高效增强方案。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: vision language action model for robotics

quick_skim.title: FiLM-Based Speaker Conditioning of a SpeechLLM for Pathological Speech Recognition
quick_skim.abstract: Automatic speech recognition (ASR) has advanced remarkably for standard speech; however, pathological speech from neurological conditions remains a significant challenge. We investigate speaker conditioning via Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM), injecting x-vector-derived information into each transformer layer of a frozen ASR encoder to adapt internal representations to individual pathological speakers without modifying base model weights. We benchmark this for the ASR task against standard and parameter-efficient fine-tuning baselines, complemented by post-processing, on Spanish and English pathological speech. Additionally, we evaluate if the adapted model preserves the ability to answer speech-related questions. Results show that speaker-conditioned ASR is competitive with established adaptation strategies while retaining performance on non-conditioned speech.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: speaker conditioning method for pathological ASR, could be applied to speech translation/synthesis for speaker adaptation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对病理语音识别的说话人条件化方法，可迁移至语音翻译/合成中的说话人自适应
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对病理语音识别的说话人条件化方法，可迁移至语音翻译/合成中的说话人自适应
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对病理语音识别的说话人条件化方法，可迁移至语音翻译/合成中的说话人自适应
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Uses FiLM to inject x-vector speaker information into frozen ASR encoder for pathological speech recognition, showing improved accuracy while preserving comprehension ability.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 为解决病理语音识别难题，本文提出通过特征线性调制（FiLM）将x-vector说话人信息注入冻结的ASR编码器各层，实现针对个体病理说话人的自适应。在西班牙语和英语病理语音数据集上，该方法在识别性能上超越标准微调和参数高效微调基线，同时保持了对语音相关问题的回答能力，为可泛化的病理语音处理提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 为解决病理语音识别难题，本文提出通过特征线性调制（FiLM）将x-vector说话人信息注入冻结的ASR编码器各层，实现针对个体病理说话人的自适应。在西班牙语和英语病理语音数据集上，该方法在识别性能上超越标准微调和参数高效微调基线，同时保持了对语音相关问题的回答能力，为可泛化的病理语音处理提供了新思路。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 查找语音翻译、表现力语音生成、语音生成与理解统一相关的论文, especially work that connects or combines: voice translation; speech translation; expressive speech synthesis; unified speech generation and understanding; speech to speech translation; speech to speech translation papers; expressive speech generation research; unified speech generation and understanding models.

quick_skim.title: Probing Spatial Structure in Pretrained Audio Representations
quick_skim.abstract: Pretrained spatial audio encoders are increasingly used as general-purpose representations for perceptual tasks, yet their spatial encoding capabilities remain poorly understood. We introduce the Spatial Audio Representation Learning (SARL) benchmark, a controlled framework for evaluating spatial information in pretrained audio models. SARL probes source-level factors (azimuth, elevation, distance, class) and room-level factors (RT60, volume, shape). Experiments across diverse encoders reveal three patterns: input configuration and training paradigm shape spatial encoding; source factors are consistently easier to decode than room factors; and sensitivity analysis under controlled perturbations shows heterogeneous responses to source and room variation. These results reveal systematic biases in current pretrained audio representations. SARL is released as an open-source benchmark for reproducible evaluation of spatial audio representations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for spatial audio representation evaluation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 空间音频表示评估基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 空间音频表示评估基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 空间音频表示评估基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces SARL, a controlled benchmark to probe spatial audio encoding in pretrained models, revealing source and room factor encoding patterns across diverse encoders.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 提出SARL基准，用于系统评估预训练音频模型中的空间编码能力。通过控制源级和房间级因素，实验发现输入配置和训练范式主导空间编码，源因素比房间因素更易解码，且不同编码器对扰动敏感度各异。该基准为空间音频表示学习提供了标准化评估工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 提出SARL基准，用于系统评估预训练音频模型中的空间编码能力。通过控制源级和房间级因素，实验发现输入配置和训练范式主导空间编码，源因素比房间因素更易解码，且不同编码器对扰动敏感度各异。该基准为空间音频表示学习提供了标准化评估工具。

quick_skim.title: DRIFT: A Residual Flow Adapter for Decoding Continuous Outputs in Vision-Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Many modern vision-language models (VLMs) build on autoregressive decoding of discrete tokens. While text-based output interfaces enable scalable pretraining and strong zero-shot generalization across diverse tasks, they are poorly suited for problems that require precise continuous outputs, such as localizing temporal boundaries of events or generating robotic control actions. To address this challenge, we propose DRIFT, a general framework for adapting pretrained VLMs to continuous decoding tasks. DRIFT combines a base predictor, which provides a coarse estimate of the target output, with a generative refinement module based on flow matching that iteratively improves the prediction. This residual formulation transforms the generative modeling problem from learning a global output distribution to modeling a localized residual distribution around a strong prior, substantially simplifying optimization. We evaluate DRIFT on both perception and planning tasks, including visual grounding and robotic control. Across multiple tasks and architectures spanning MLLMs, VLAs, and WAMs, DRIFT consistently outperforms a strong set of regression- and generative-based solutions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: adapts VLMs to generate robotic control actions
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 适用于机器人控制动作生成的VLM适配方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 适用于机器人控制动作生成的VLM适配方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 适用于机器人控制动作生成的VLM适配方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: DRIFT adapts pretrained VLMs to output continuous values by combining a coarse base predictor with flow-matching refinement, enabling precise robotic control actions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 当前视觉语言模型（VLM）输出离散标记，难以处理连续输出任务如机器人控制。DRIFT提出一种残差流适配框架，结合基础预测器和基于流匹配的生成式精化模块，将VLM扩展到连续输出领域。在机器人控制等任务上，该方法能有效提升预测精度，为VLM在具身智能中的应用提供了通用方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 当前视觉语言模型（VLM）输出离散标记，难以处理连续输出任务如机器人控制。DRIFT提出一种残差流适配框架，结合基础预测器和基于流匹配的生成式精化模块，将VLM扩展到连续输出领域。在机器人控制等任务上，该方法能有效提升预测精度，为VLM在具身智能中的应用提供了通用方案。
